refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined multiple obligations: analyzing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these are separated into distinct sub-FRs focusing on single aspects of worksite analysis without implying how the analysis is performed.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and present an analysis of the terrain independently of obstacle and work-zone data, through test cases or demonstrations.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of obstacles within the worksite area.
The NGHE solution shall provide representation of the work-zone within the worksite area.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital representation of the terrain features relevant to the worksite
Extract and classify key terrain attributes to form a structured terrain model
Create a geospatial map integrating terrain data with safety and operational constraints
Develop an abstract terrain feature set accessible via a standardized interface for planning and execution
selected_dp.selected_dp: Generate a digital representation of the terrain features relevant to the worksite

potential_sources: Integration of safety plan and operational policies into terrain model generation
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on creating a digital terrain representation that can be used independently by planning and execution functions, supporting modularity and minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain data sources
Risk that safety and operational policies may require frequent updates impacting terrain model consistency
Assumes planning and execution functions can consume the terrain model in agreed formats
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining interfaces for terrain model access to ensure decoupling between terrain analysis and downstream functions

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for processing and generating digital representations of terrain features relevant to the worksite

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives raw sensor data and environmental inputs relevant to terrain features

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands or parameters defining the scope or focus of terrain data generation

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Sends processed digital terrain representations to other system modules or external systems

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as terrain data processing is core to NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion as output interfaces are well defined.
Vehicle module primarily handles mobility and control; terrain data generation would diffuse significantly outside this module.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control and monitoring; terrain data generation is peripheral and would cause high diffusion.
Safety Services is unrelated to terrain data generation; containment is very weak and diffusion very high.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the DP 'Generate a digital representation of the terrain features relevant to the worksite' because it specializes in processing environmental data and generating digital models, resulting in high cohesion and low diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on raw sensor data interfaces that may change, requiring interface stability management.
Risk of overloading NGHE Solution with unrelated processing tasks if scope is not well managed.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Generate a digital representation of the terrain features relevant to the worksite' directly satisfies the FR 'The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain' by specifying the creation of a digital terrain model, which is a clear and verifiable functional solution.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on raw sensor data interfaces that may change, requiring interface stability management.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion (containment score 8) and low diffusion (diffusion score 2), indicating that the DP is well contained within a single implementation module. Other modules have low containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module receives raw sensor data and tasking inputs and outputs processed digital terrain representations, which can be validated against acceptance criteria requiring a worksite model/map available to planning and execution functions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and well justified by the module's scope and containment scores, ensuring ownership and responsibility for the FR are well defined.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and manage dependencies on raw sensor data interfaces to maintain interface stability.
Ensure the NGHE Solution module scope remains focused on terrain data processing to avoid overloading with unrelated tasks.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear justifications and manageable risks. The DP directly satisfies the FR, the DP is well contained within a single IM with high cohesion and low diffusion, and verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Recommended revisions focus on monitoring dependencies and maintaining module scope, which are operational considerations rather than reasons to further decompose. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and stable module boundary is essential, and the current decomposition level supports this well.

revise.why: Monitor and manage dependencies on raw sensor data interfaces to maintain interface stability and ensure the NGHE Solution module scope remains focused on terrain data processing to avoid overloading with unrelated tasks.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can effectively manage interface stability risks.
Verification methods for digital terrain representations are reliable and accepted.
open_risks: Potential dependency on raw sensor data interfaces that may change, requiring interface stability management.
evidence_needed: Verification results confirming the digital terrain model meets acceptance criteria.
Interface stability monitoring reports for raw sensor data inputs.
